prompt_rules: eight-frame motion study for ONE direction only, arranged in a 4x2 grid: TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right
high-resolution 16-bit-plus pixel art, each frame about 256x256, character drawn LARGE filling most of the cell height with a small margin
follow the supplied motion phases exactly
same character identity, facing and scale in every cell

prompt:
Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER IDLE (breathing) sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing UP (back view: back of head, tiny low ponytail with vermilion cord, back of the indigo haori; no face). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions and body width of the attached reference (his own walking sheet) EXACTLY - same head-to-body ratio, same slim-but-sturdy balance, same total height. NOT wider, NOT stockier than the reference, EXACTLY the same head size, total height and body width as the front view. Standing relaxed and natural, feet CLOSE together about shoulder-width apart, legs nearly vertical - NOT a wide stance - and the feet NEVER move. 8-frame subtle BREATHING LOOP: frame1 = neutral stance, frame2 = slow inhale, shoulders rise 1-2px, frame3 = inhale peak, frame4 = breath held, the ponytail sways, frame5 = exhale begins, frame6 = lowest relaxed point 1-2px lower, frame7 = body returns toward neutral, the haori back panel sways, frame8 = back to neutral so the loop repeats seamlessly. The silhouette change between frames is SMALL and smooth. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
